## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On July 17, 1918, the incredibly massive, brutally horrific, and historically definitive execution of the Romanov family aggressively occurred in Yekaterinburg, Russia. Following the absolute collapse of the Russian Empire and the massive Bolshevik Revolution, Tsar Nicholas II, his wife Alexandra, and their five children were aggressively herded into a basement and brutally slaughtered by a massive Bolshevik firing squad. This incredibly bloody assassination fundamentally and absolutely eradicated the 300-year-old Romanov dynasty, fiercely sealing the absolute victory of Vladimir Lenin's communist regime.

On July 17, 1945, the massive, highly historic, and geopolitically definitive Potsdam Conference officially and aggressively commenced in Germany. The "Big Three" Allied leaders—Harry S. Truman, Winston Churchill, and Joseph Stalin—convened to heavily negotiate the massive post-war administration of a completely defeated Nazi Germany. The massive, deeply contentious agreements reached at Potsdam aggressively established the absolute, hard borders of post-war Europe, heavily solidifying the massive ideological divide that would immediately and fiercely trigger the Cold War.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On July 17, 1996, the incredibly massive, culturally iconic, and fiercely historic southern Indian metropolis of Madras was officially and aggressively renamed Chennai. The state government of Tamil Nadu, heavily led by Chief Minister M. Karunanidhi, executed the massive name change to aggressively shed the city's absolute, deeply entrenched British colonial legacy. The massive, deeply symbolic reversion to its historical, indigenous identity heavily reflected the massive, ongoing nationwide movement to decolonize the absolute cultural and geographical nomenclature of the Indian republic.

On July 17, 1489, Bahlul Lodi, the massive, incredibly formidable, and fiercely strategic founder of the Lodi dynasty, officially passed away. Having aggressively conquered the massive remnants of the Sayyid dynasty, he successfully established the absolute final ruling dynasty of the massive Delhi Sultanate. His incredible, massive military campaigns heavily consolidated Afghan power across northern India, deeply setting the massive, historical stage for the eventual, aggressive confrontation between the Lodi dynasty and the invading Mughal forces of Babur decades later.
